summary: Use --setting-sources '' to isolate conditions from user-installed plugins in CLI agent sessions
context: Run 9be7a749 showed ALL conditions (including baseline) using 168+ Twining tool calls. The CLI loads user-installed plugins by default, contaminating the experimental design. Baseline was effectively 'baseline + Twining', invalidating the entire comparison.
rationale: --setting-sources '' starts with no auto-loaded plugins while preserving OAuth auth (unlike --bare which breaks auth). Conditions that need plugins explicitly add them via --plugin-dir. Verified: baseline gets zero Twining tools, full-twining gets all 32.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Skips OAuth/keychain auth, requires explicit ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Most subscription users don't have API keys.
Only controls MCP servers, not plugins. Twining loads as a plugin, not a standalone MCP server.
